title: One Connection Act This Week
subtitle: A simple activity for making one concrete move toward a relationship you have been neglecting
excerpt: A practical activity for the adult friendships that have been meaning to happen but haven't. Asks for one specific connection act this week: a message sent, a call made, a plan confirmed. Not a big event. One concrete step.

practice.whenToUse: Use when there is a friendship or relationship that has been drifting and you keep meaning to do something about it but have not.

content.text: A single-action activity for the relationships you have been meaning to tend. Adult friendships do not maintain themselves. A relationship that has gone quiet for lack of contact needs one person to break the quiet. This week, that person is you.
This week, do one specific thing that moves a relationship forward. Name the person before you begin. Name the specific action. Do it by Friday.
A voice message that is more than a check-in.
A specific plan made: a date, a time, a place. Not 'we should catch up soon'.
A card or letter sent by post to someone you have been thinking about.
Calling someone you haven't spoken to in months because you have been meaning to.

Adult friendships require more deliberate maintenance than childhood or early-adult ones, because the structural scaffolding that creates automatic contact, shared classes, shared workplaces, shared geography, has largely gone. Without that scaffolding, friendship is maintained by intentional acts. One act changes nothing on its own. One act a week, consistently, rebuilds the connection over time.
Name the person. Name the act. Do it this week.
